message:
sdk: bump python + node to 2.3.0-alpha.1 + release prep doc (issue #5)

Aligns the PyPI and npm SDK crates with the root rulake crate
(ADR-157) so the v2.3 publish is a one-line operation once root
2.3.0 goes stable.

- python/Cargo.toml: rulake-py 2.2.0 -> 2.3.0-alpha.1
- python/pyproject.toml: rulake (PyPI) 2.2.0 -> 2.3.0-alpha.1
- node/Cargo.toml: rulake-node 2.2.0 -> 2.3.0-alpha.1
- node/package.json: rulake (npm) 2.2.0 -> 2.3.0-alpha.1
  + per-platform optionalDependencies and rulake-wasm pin
- docs/deploy/v2.3-release-prep.md: full crate inventory, publish
  runbook (crates.io / PyPI / npm), token sourcing from GCP Secrets

Both release builds verified green:
  cargo build --release --manifest-path python/Cargo.toml
  cargo build --release --manifest-path node/Cargo.toml

Publish itself is GATED on root rulake 2.3.0 stable (currently
2.3.0-alpha.1). No publish step run here.

Co-Authored-By: claude-flow <ruv@ruv.net>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,169 @@
+# v2.3 Release Prep
+
+Tracks the version-bump prep work for the **v2.3 cut**. This document is the
+hand-off for the operator who runs the actual publish (gated on the root
+`rulake` crate going `2.3.0` stable — currently `2.3.0-alpha.1`).
+
+Issue: [#5 — PyPI + npm SDK republish at v2.3](https://github.com/ruvnet/RuLake/issues/5)
+Branch: `stretch/v2.3-rollout`
+
+---
+
+## 1. Crate version inventory
+
+Target for this rollout is `2.3.0-alpha.1`. The two SDK crates were stuck on
+`2.2.0` and have been bumped in this commit. The backend crates ride on their
+own `0.x` lifecycle (see ADR-005 for `ipfs-backend`, ADR-004 for `gcs-backend`,
+ADRs for the MCP tier) and are intentionally **not** rebadged to 2.3 — they
+ship independently.
+
+| Crate                          | Manifest                          | Version          | At 2.3.0-alpha.1? | Notes                                                       |
+| ------------------------------ | --------------------------------- | ---------------- | ----------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------- |
+| `rulake` (root)                | `Cargo.toml`                      | `2.3.0-alpha.1`  | yes               | Source of truth. ADR-157 — promote to stable when kernel ships. |
+| `rulake-py`                    | `python/Cargo.toml`               | `2.3.0-alpha.1`  | yes (this commit) | PyO3 binding (ADR-002).                                      |
+| `rulake` (PyPI dist)           | `python/pyproject.toml`           | `2.3.0-alpha.1`  | yes (this commit) | Maturin build; matches `rulake-py`.                          |
+| `rulake-node`                  | `node/Cargo.toml`                 | `2.3.0-alpha.1`  | yes (this commit) | napi-rs binding (ADR-003).                                   |
+| `rulake` (npm dist)            | `node/package.json`               | `2.3.0-alpha.1`  | yes (this commit) | optionalDependencies for per-platform `.node` artifacts also bumped to `2.3.0-alpha.1`, including `rulake-wasm`. |
+| `rulake-wasm`                  | `node-wasm/Cargo.toml` + `package.json` | `2.3.0-alpha.1`  | yes               | Already matched root.                                        |
+| `mcp-server`                   | `mcp-server/Cargo.toml`           | `0.8.0`          | no — independent  | Ships on its own cadence (last cut: per-call caps + backpressure). |
+| `mcp-rvdna`                    | `mcp-rvdna/Cargo.toml`            | `0.0.1`          | no — independent  | Pre-1.0 connector.                                           |
+| `mcp-ruqu`                     | `mcp-ruqu/Cargo.toml`             | `0.1.0`          | no — independent  | Pre-1.0 connector.                                           |
+| `rvdna-backend`                | `rvdna-backend/Cargo.toml`        | `0.2.0`          | no — independent  | Backend on its own minor cadence.                            |
+| `ruqu-backend`                 | `ruqu-backend/Cargo.toml`         | `0.2.0`          | no — independent  | Backend on its own minor cadence.                            |
+| `gcs-backend`                  | `gcs-backend/Cargo.toml`          | `0.1.0`          | no — independent  | ADR-004; pre-1.0.                                            |
+| `ipfs-backend`                 | `ipfs-backend/Cargo.toml`         | `0.1.0`          | no — independent  | ADR-005; pre-1.0.                                            |
+
+**Drift summary**: no drift inside the "v2.3 family" (root, python, node,
+node-wasm). Backend + MCP crates are deliberately decoupled and stay at their
+own versions.
+
+---
+
+## 2. Build verification (this commit)
+
+| Crate          | Command                                                  | Status         |
+| -------------- | -------------------------------------------------------- | -------------- |
+| `rulake-py`    | `cargo build --release --manifest-path python/Cargo.toml`| green          |
+| `rulake-node`  | `cargo build --release --manifest-path node/Cargo.toml`  | green          |
+
+Run again before publish to be sure nothing rotted:
+
+```bash
+cargo build --release --manifest-path python/Cargo.toml
+cargo build --release --manifest-path node/Cargo.toml
+```
+
+---
+
+## 3. Publish runbook (DO NOT RUN until root v2.3.0 stable)
+
+> **Gate**: root `Cargo.toml` must read `version = "2.3.0"` (no `-alpha`
+> suffix). When that lands, drop the `-alpha.1` suffix from each manifest in
+> §1 and re-run the builds before publishing.
+
+### 3a. Crates.io (root + standalone backends)
+
+`crates.io` requires deps to be published before dependents. The root crate
+has no `crates.io` deps inside this repo (the vendored `ruvector-rabitq` is
+already published, and the path-deps to backends are dev/integration only),
+so root publishes first; the SDK crates have `publish = false` and never go
+to `crates.io`.
+
+Publish order (skip any crate already at the target version on crates.io):
+
+```bash
+# 1. root — must go first (everything else may depend on it)
+cargo publish --manifest-path Cargo.toml
+
+# 2. backends (independent versioning; only republish if the version changed)
+cargo publish --manifest-path gcs-backend/Cargo.toml
+cargo publish --manifest-path ipfs-backend/Cargo.toml
+cargo publish --manifest-path rvdna-backend/Cargo.toml
+cargo publish --manifest-path ruqu-backend/Cargo.toml
+
+# 3. MCP tier (each is a separate crate, independent versioning)
+cargo publish --manifest-path mcp-server/Cargo.toml
+cargo publish --manifest-path mcp-rvdna/Cargo.toml
+cargo publish --manifest-path mcp-ruqu/Cargo.toml
+```
+
+Note: `python/Cargo.toml` and `node/Cargo.toml` both set `publish = false` —
+they ride PyPI and npm, not crates.io.
+
+### 3b. PyPI (Python SDK)
+
+```bash
+cd python && maturin publish --interpreter python3.12
+```
+
+`maturin publish` builds the wheel + sdist, then uploads via the PyPI API.
+Requires `MATURIN_PYPI_TOKEN` (or the global `PYPI_TOKEN`) in the environment
+— see §4.
+
+Verify after upload:
+
+```bash
+pip install rulake==2.3.0
+python -c "import rulake; print(rulake.__version__)"
+```
+
+### 3c. npm (Node SDK)
+
+```bash
+cd node && npx napi build --platform --release && npm publish --access=public --tag=alpha
+```
+
+The `--tag=alpha` is correct **only** while the root crate is `2.3.0-alpha.x`.
+For the stable `2.3.0` cut, drop `--tag=alpha` so the release lands on the
+default `latest` dist-tag.
+
+Each per-platform `.node` artifact (`rulake-linux-x64-gnu`,
+`rulake-linux-arm64-gnu`, `rulake-darwin-x64`, `rulake-darwin-arm64`,
+`rulake-win32-x64-msvc`) is a separate npm package and must be published
+from its own platform-built binary — `napi-rs` does this automatically when
+`--platform` is set and `NPM_TOKEN` is in the environment.
+
+Verify after upload:
+
+```bash
+npm install rulake@2.3.0-alpha.1
+node -e "console.log(require('rulake'))"
+```
+
+---
+
+## 4. Tokens
+
+All publish credentials live in **GCP Secrets Manager** (project follows the
+`rulake-prod` convention used by the deploy pipeline — see `docs/deploy/cloud-run.md`).
+
+| Secret name        | Used by                          | Scope                               |
+| ------------------ | -------------------------------- | ----------------------------------- |
+| `CRATES_API_KEY`   | `cargo publish` (§3a)            | crates.io publish, owner-level token. |
+| `PYPI_TOKEN`       | `maturin publish` (§3b)          | PyPI project-scoped token for `rulake`. Also accepted via `MATURIN_PYPI_TOKEN`. |
+| `NPM_TOKEN`        | `npm publish` (§3c)              | npm automation token for the `rulake` org. |
+
+Pull them at publish time, e.g.:
+
+```bash
+export CRATES_API_KEY=$(gcloud secrets versions access latest --secret=CRATES_API_KEY)
+export PYPI_TOKEN=$(gcloud secrets versions access latest --secret=PYPI_TOKEN)
+export NPM_TOKEN=$(gcloud secrets versions access latest --secret=NPM_TOKEN)
+```
+
+Never commit any of these. `.env` files containing these values must stay
+out of git (CLAUDE.md rule).
+
+---
+
+## 5. Hand-off checklist
+
+- [ ] Root `Cargo.toml` is at `2.3.0` (no `-alpha`).
+- [ ] All four SDK manifests (python `Cargo.toml`/`pyproject.toml`,
+      node `Cargo.toml`/`package.json`) updated to match.
+- [ ] `node/package.json` `optionalDependencies` versions match the new SDK version.
+- [ ] Both release builds succeed.
+- [ ] Tokens fetched from GCP Secrets Manager into the publish shell.
+- [ ] Publish in order: crates.io root → PyPI → npm.
+- [ ] `pip install rulake==2.3.0` and `npm install rulake@2.3.0` smoke-tested.
+- [ ] Issue #5 closed with version + dist-tag links.

-version = "2.2.0"
+version = "2.3.0-alpha.1"

  "hex",
  "rand",
@@ -400,7 +400,7 @@ dependencies = [

-version = "2.2.0"
+version = "2.3.0-alpha.1"

  "napi",
  "napi-build",

-version = "2.2.0"
+version = "2.3.0-alpha.1"
 edition = "2021"
 license = "MIT OR Apache-2.0"
 authors = ["Ruvector Team"]
@@ -21,7 +21,7 @@ publish = false

-rulake = { path = "..", version = "2.2.0" }

 napi = { version = "2.16", default-features = false, features = ["napi8", "async"] }
 napi-derive = "2.16"

-version = "2.2.0"

  "hex",
  "rand",
@@ -429,7 +429,7 @@ dependencies = [

@@ -9,7 +9,7 @@

-version = "2.2.0"
+version = "2.3.0-alpha.1"
 edition = "2021"
 license = "MIT OR Apache-2.0"
 authors = ["Ruvector Team"]
@@ -27,7 +27,7 @@ crate-type = ["cdylib"]
 [dependencies]
 # The cache + router crate one directory up. This is the whole point —
 # every `pyo3` wrapper here is a thin shim over the public Rust surface.
-rulake = { path = "..", version = "2.2.0" }
+rulake = { path = "..", version = "2.3.0-alpha.1" }
 # PyO3 — abi3-py39 keeps one wheel working across CPython 3.9–3.13.
 # The `extension-module` feature is required when building a Python
 # extension (otherwise we'd link libpython at build time, breaking

@@ -11,7 +11,7 @@ build-backend = "maturin"
 
 [project]
 name = "rulake"
-version = "2.2.0"
+version = "2.3.0-alpha.1"
 description = "Memory Lake for Agentic AI — fast, witness-anchored vector memory for LLM agents. Cache layer for RAG, semantic search, federated retrieval over S3/BigQuery/Parquet, MCP-compatible. Python bindings."
 readme = "README.md"
 requires-python = ">=3.9"
